claims.claim_text: OPA and Gatekeeper are common tools for Kubernetes admission policy.
claims.span_hint: line 459 | section: 3. Control Plane Architecture and Guardrails
claims.anchor_text: OPA and Gatekeeper are common for Kubernetes admission policy, but the principle is broader than one tool.
claims.suggested_rewrite: OPA and Gatekeeper are examples of tools used for Kubernetes admission policy, but the principle is broader than one tool.
claims.rationale: Allowlisted upstream sources can support what OPA and Gatekeeper are, but not the prevalence claim that they are 'common' in practice.

claims.claim_text: Crossplane supports claim-based custom APIs so users can request platform resources through Kubernetes-style objects.
claims.span_hint: line 634 | section: 4. Provisioning Engines and Implementation Approaches
claims.rationale: Crossplane's official documentation directly explains claims and how they expose custom APIs, but docs.crossplane.io is outside the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Crossplane reconciles composite resources and surfaces status conditions on them.
claims.span_hint: line 634 | section: 4. Provisioning Engines and Implementation Approaches
claims.rationale: The official Crossplane docs describe reconciliation and the Synced/Ready conditions on composite resources, but the host is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Crossplane compositions let platform teams expose a simplified custom resource that creates provider resources behind the scenes.
claims.span_hint: line 634 | section: 4. Provisioning Engines and Implementation Approaches
claims.rationale: The official Composition docs directly show a custom resource driving creation of provider-managed resources, but docs.crossplane.io is not allowlisted.

claims.claim_text: Self-service infrastructure predates modern cloud portals.
claims.anchor_text: Mainframe, database, and enterprise IT teams used request catalogs long before public cloud, but modern platforms add automated validation, provisioning, lifecycle operations, and policy-as-code.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Self-service infrastructure predates modern cloud portals, but modern platforms add automated validation, provisioning, lifecycle operations, and policy-as-code.
claims.rationale: The teaching point is sound, but the specific historical framing about mainframe and database teams would need a stronger source than the current allowlist provides.

claims.claim_text: Crossplane can use Kubernetes-style APIs to manage non-Kubernetes infrastructure.
claims.rationale: Crossplane's official docs directly describe composing non-Kubernetes resources behind Kubernetes-style APIs, but the canonical docs host is off-allowlist.

further_reading.title: Crossplane GitHub repository
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful upstream entry point for the control-plane and composition model discussed throughout the module.

further_reading.title: Kubernetes Docs: Custom Resources
further_reading.why_relevant: Explains the Kubernetes API-extension model that underpins several self-service infrastructure patterns in the module.

further_reading.title: Gatekeeper GitHub repository
further_reading.why_relevant: Concrete upstream reference for policy and admission-control patterns mentioned in the guardrails section.

notes: Seeded only prose claims that make named historical or tool-capability assertions; excluded diagrams, code blocks, quiz items, exercise scaffolds, and illustrative tables.
